name: U.S. Rep. Stephanie Murphy
tweet: ...I’m optimistic about the future. But it won’t be easy. I’ll keep working in Congress to build bipartisan support to treat gun violence like the epidemic it is. We must work together, and we must act like our lives - and our children’s lives - depend on it. Because they do.

name: U.S. Rep. Stephanie Murphy
tweet: ...I refuse to accept these mass shootings - and the daily drumbeat of gun violence in our communities - as the new normal. From Pulse to Parkland to Las Vegas, I’m continually inspired by the survivors and victims of these tragedies, their stories, and their advocacy...

name: U.S. Rep. Stephanie Murphy
tweet: ...We don’t have all the answers, but we do have some, and some is a start. What this Congress lacks, however, is the courage to stand up to the corporate gun lobby and pass even incremental, popular reforms. It’s shameful. We deserve better. Our children deserve better...

name: U.S. Rep. Stephanie Murphy
tweet: ...We must do more than simply pay lip service to mental health; we need to strengthen it and better fund it. Weapons should be harder to get, not easier. Assault weapons that can take down hundreds in a manner of minutes belong on battlefields, not on our streets...

name: U.S. Rep. Stephanie Murphy
tweet: ...After each of these tragedies, we looked for a single deficiency to “solve” this problem because it was easier than identifying the real problem: we have a systemic gun violence problem in this country. We need more than patchwork fixes; we need comprehensive reform...

name: U.S. Rep. Stephanie Murphy
tweet: One year ago today, hundreds of individuals & families had their lives taken or forever changed in the #LasVegasShooting. My thoughts are with them today. Since then, in Florida alone, we’ve had mass shootings in Parkland, Jacksonville, and before that, Pulse. Enough is enough...
